channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Access blocked by landowner, 17th June
item.description:
The footpath sign has been broken off, the gate is padlocked and covered in barbed wire to prevent access to the footpath. USRN : 40103955 Street : BADGEWORTH FOOTPATH 23 Number : ABA23 Type : FP

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Badgeworth Road, Badgeworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Kerbs and Channels problem, 17th June
item.description:
Give and take 28day please lift and relay x1 rocking raised kerb dropped stone 900x100x35mm stats required (CW Edge Kerbing & Channels)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Other FW6 Surface problem, 17th June
item.description:

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 17th June
item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 17th June
item.description:
3 way stop and go 28 days temporary footway closure please cut back overgrown tree branches growing out into carriage way and footway approx 2 meters x3 meters number 2 (Overgrown Tree)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown, 17th June
item.description:
When we walk in the morning to school, it’s so difficult to walk around this area, as bikes are riding fast, we had a incident when a bike nearly hit us while walking , it’s a blindspot for bikes , and it nearly hitting us mummy is a issue!!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Discovery Road, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 17th June
item.description:
Dear Gloucestershire Highways, Our architectural practice has recently concluded an adaptation project at the address: 19 lambert Drive, Shurdington, Cheltenham, GL51 4SP, and I am reaching out to ask if resurfacing the tarmac on an existing dropped kerb pavement requires planning approval prior to commencing work? As after looking on the vehicle dropped kerb application, it asks for prior planning approval however, as the dropped kerb is already there but requires resurfacing is an application still requires and if so, do we need planning approval still? If you could please advise that would be greatly appreciated Kind Regards,
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 17th June
item.description:
NWD Road closure carriage way pothole approx 800x600x70mm plus x 1 NSD (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 17th June
item.description:
Give and take 28 day plus temporary footway closure please replace x5 broken sunken 750x600x20mm slabs stats required (FW Broken Slabs)
Report on FixMyStreet
